potential_hallucinations.requirement: The system shall allow a user to log in by entering their username and password.
The system shall display the requested information once the user selects an option.
The system shall allow a police user to log in by entering their username and password.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: A user starts by entering their username and password. If the details are incorrect, they try again until they are able to log in.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall allow a user to try again if the login details are incorrect until successful login.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall display the requested information once the user selects an option.
hallucinations.reason: The source text does not specify that the system will display any specific type of information after a user selects an option. It only mentions that 'the requested information is shown'.

duplicates.requirement_a: The system shall allow a user to try again if the login details are incorrect until successful login.
duplicates.requirement_b: The system shall allow the police user to try again if the login details are incorrect until successful login.
duplicates.reason: These two requirements are identical and should be considered duplicates.

ambiguities.rewritten_requirement: The system shall display the requested information once the user selects an option.
ambiguities.reason: It is unclear what 'the requested information' refers to in this context. The source text does not provide specific details about the type of information that should be displayed.

final_assessment_policy: moderate: needs_review if extraction/rewriting failed, no rewritten requirements were produced, exact duplicate rewritten requirements were detected, or the LLM validator reports 6 or more total issues across missing requirements, hallucinations, duplicates, and ambiguities
